2605.23590.title: Co-ReAct: Rubrics as Step-Level Collaborators for ReAct Agents
2605.23590.paper_summary: ReAct-style agents for search-intensive, multi-step reasoning tasks rely largely on their own internal judgment to decide what evidence to seek, which reasoning or action step to take next, and when to stop, often producing shallow, redundant, or poorly targeted trajectories. Prior work has explored rubrics as external quality signals, but existing uses are mostly evaluative rather than action-guiding: rubrics typically serve as training-time rewards or post-hoc evaluators of completed outputs, and in deep-research settings they are often coarse-grained and report-level rather than step-level. We introduce Co-ReAct, a rubric-guided action-selection framework that uses rubrics as step-level guidance during inference. At each decision step, Co-ReAct injects a rubric into the agent's context to guide the next Reason-or-Act decision, specifying what the agent should target in evidence seeking, search, reasoning, or self-evaluation. To make this guidance reliable, we train a dedicated rubric generator with GRPO. Unlike prior pairwise or binary preference formulations, our objective optimizes a list-wise Spearman rank-correlation reward against multi-judge expert consensus rankings, encouraging rubrics that are discriminative rather than merely plausible. On DeepResearchBench and SQA-CS-V2, Co-ReAct consistently improves over ReAct and representative test-time compute baselines across search agents built on both 8B/14B open-source and frontier closed-source base models. The trained rubric generator can also serve as a drop-in component that improves these baselines without changing their underlying decision mechanisms. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/ZBWpro/Co-ReAct.
2605.23590.paper_summary_zh: ReAct風格的代理在搜索密集型、多步推理任務中主要依賴其自身的內部判斷來決定尋找什麼證據、下一步該採取什麼推理或行動步驟，以及何時停止，這常常導致產生淺薄、冗餘或目標不明的軌跡。先前的研究探討了作為外部質量信號的評分標準，但現有的使用主要是評估性的，而非指導行動的：評分標準通常作為訓練時的獎勵或對已完成輸出的事後評估者，在深度研究環境中，它們往往是粗糙的報告級別，而非步驟級別。我們介紹了Co-ReAct，一種以評分標準為指導的行動選擇框架，該框架在推理過程中使用評分標準作為步驟級指導。在每個決策步驟中，Co-ReAct將評分標準注入代理的上下文中，以指導下一個推理或行動決策，具體說明代理在尋找證據、搜索、推理或自我評估中應該針對什麼。為了使這種指導可靠，我們使用GRPO訓練了一個專用的評分標準生成器。與先前的成對或二元偏好公式不同，我們的目標是針對多位專家共識排名優化列表式的Spearman等級相關獎勵，鼓勵生成具有區分性的評分標準，而不僅僅是看似合理的。在DeepResearchBench和SQA-CS-V2上，Co-ReAct在基於8B/14B開源和前沿閉源基礎模型構建的搜索代理上，始終優於ReAct和代表性的測試時計算基準。訓練好的評分標準生成器還可以作為一個即插即用的組件，改善這些基準而不改變其底層決策機制。我們的代碼已公開於 https://github.com/ZBWpro/Co-ReAct。
2605.23590.author: Jiazheng Kang et.al.
2605.23590.authors: Jiazheng Kang, Bowen Zhang, Zixin Song, Jiangwang Chen, Xiao Yang, Da Zhu, Guanjun Jiang
